input.prompt:
I need to create a C# MCP server that uses HTTP transport for deployment
as a web service. It should expose both tools and resources (for example,
a resource that returns configuration data). Show me how to set up the
HTTP transport with MapMcp and implement a resource.

I need to create a C# MCP server that uses HTTP transport for deployment
as a web service. It should expose both tools and resources (for example,
a resource that returns configuration data). Show me how to set up the
HTTP transport with MapMcp and implement a resource.

arguments.name: explore-mcp-api
arguments.prompt:
I need to understand the MCP C# library API for creating an HTTP server with tools and resources. Look at these XML doc files and DLLs to find:

1. How to use `MapMcp` - what extension methods exist in ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore
2. How to define resources using attributes (like `[McpServerResource]` or similar)
3. How to define tools using attributes (like `[McpServerTool]` or similar)
4. What the `McpResourceTemplate` and `McpResource` types look like
5. How `AddMcpServer()` is configured

Check these files:
- /home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.xml
- /home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml

Also check the DLL public types:
```
dotnet tool install --global ILSpy.Tool 2>/dev/null || true
```

Or alternatively, just grep through the XML files for key terms: MapMcp, McpServerResource, McpServerTool, McpResource, McpResourceTemplate, AddMcpServer, ReadResource, ResourceTemplate, WithHttpTransport, WithStatelessHttpTransport

Show me all relevant API signatures and attribute definitions.

<member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})">
            <summary>
            Adds the services necessary for <see cref="M:McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp"/>
            to handle MCP requests and sessions using the MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
            </summary>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <param name="configureOptions">Configures options for the Streamable HTTP transport. This allows configuring per-session
            <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions"/> and running logic before and after a session.</param>
            <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
            <remarks>For more information on configuring the underlying HTTP server
            to control things like port binding and custom TLS certificates, see the <see href="https://learn.microsoft.com/aspnet/core/fundamentals/minimal-apis">Minimal APIs quick reference</see>.
            </remarks>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.AddAuthorizationFilters(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder)">
            <summary>
            Adds authorization filters to support <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.AuthorizeAttribute"/>
            on MCP server tools, prompts, and resources. This method should always be called when using
            ASP.NET Core integration to ensure proper authorization support.
            </summary>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            This method automatically configures authorization filters for all MCP server handlers. These filters respect
            authorization attributes such as <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.AuthorizeAttribute"/>
            and <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.AllowAnonymousAttribute"/>.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithDistributedCacheEventStreamStore(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions})">
            <summary>
            Registers a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore"/> as the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.ISseEventStreamStore"/> for SSE resumability.
            </summary>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <param name="configureOptions">An optional action to configure <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions"/>.</param>
            <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Distributed.IDistributedCache"/> implementation must be registered in the service collection before calling this method.
            The registered cache is automatically assigned to <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.Cache"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            To use a specific <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Distributed.IDistributedCache"/> instance instead of the one registered in DI,
            set the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.Cache"/> property in the <paramref name="configureOptions"/> callback.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions">
            <summary>
            Provides extension methods for <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder"/> to add MCP endpoints.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String)">
            <summary>
            Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
            </summary>
            <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
            <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
            <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The required MCP services have not been registered. Ensure <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})"/> has been called during application startup.</exception>
            <remarks>
            For details about the Streamable HTTP transport, see the <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports#streamable-http">2025-11-25 protocol specification</see>.
            When legacy SSE is enabled via <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.EnableLegacySse"/>, this method also maps legacy SSE endpoints at the path "/sse" and "/message". For details about the HTTP with SSE transport, see the <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">2024-11-05 protocol specification</see>.

import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

# Parse the XML files
aspnetcore_xml = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.xml'
core_xml = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml'

def print_member_doc(xml_file, member_names):
    tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
    root = tree.getroot()
    
    for member_name in member_names:
        members = root.findall(f"./members/member[@name='{member_name}']")
        if members:
            for member in members:
                print(f"\n{'='*80}")
                print(f"Member: {member_name}")
                print(f"{'='*80}")
                # Print the XML string
                xml_str = ET.tostring(member, encoding='unicode')
                # Pretty print
                from xml.dom import minidom
                dom = minidom.parseString(xml_str)
                print(dom.toprettyxml(indent="  "))

# Look for MapMcp
print("\n\n### MAPPING MCP ###")
print_member_doc(aspnetcore_xml, [
    "M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String)"
])

# Look for WithHttpTransport
print("\n\n### WITH HTTP TRANSPORT ###")
print_member_doc(aspnetcore_xml, [
    "M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})"
])

EOF

  <summary>
            Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
            </summary>
  
            
  <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
  
            
  <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
  
            
  <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
  
            
  <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">

    <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})"/>
     has been called during application startup.
  </exception>
  
            
  <remarks>
    
            For details about the Streamable HTTP transport, see the 
    <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports#streamable-http">2025-11-25 protocol specification</see>
    .
            When legacy SSE is enabled via 
    <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.EnableLegacySse"/>
    , this method also maps legacy SSE endpoints at the path "/sse" and "/message". For details about the HTTP with SSE transport, see the

python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

core_xml = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml'

def find_and_print_types(xml_file, search_terms):
    tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
    root = tree.getroot()
    
    # Find all members
    for member in root.findall("./members/member"):
        name = member.get('name', '')
        # Check if any search term matches
        for term in search_terms:
            if term in name and name.startswith('T:'):
                print(f"\nFOUND: {name}")
                xml_str = ET.tostring(member, encoding='unicode')
                from xml.dom import minidom
                dom = minidom.parseString(xml_str)
                print(dom.toprettyxml(indent="  ")[:1500])  # Limit output
                break

# Search for attribute and type definitions
search_terms = [
    'McpServerToolAttribute',
    'McpServerResourceAttribute', 
    'McpServerToolTypeAttribute',
    'McpServerResourceTypeAttribute',
    'McpServerTool',
    'McpServerResource',
    'McpResourceTemplate'
]

find_and_print_types(core_xml, search_terms)

EOF

import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import re

core_xml = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml'

tree = ET.parse(core_xml)
root = tree.getroot()

# Find all members that are types (start with T:)

target_names = [
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute',
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceAttribute',
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolTypeAttribute',
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceTypeAttribute',
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool',
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource'
]

for member in root.findall("./members/member"):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    if name in target_names:
        summary = member.find('summary')
        remarks = member.find('remarks')
        print(f"\n{'='*80}")
        print(f"Type: {name}")
        print(f"{'='*80}")
        if summary is not None:
            text = ''.join(summary.itertext()).strip()
            print(f"Summary: {text}")
        if remarks is not None:
            text = ''.join(remarks.itertext()).strip()
            print(f"Remarks: {text[:500]}")

target_names = [
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute',
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceAttribute',
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolTypeAttribute',
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceTypeAttribute',
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool',
    'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource'
]

for member in root.findall("./members/member"):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    if name in target_names:
        summary = member.find('summary')
        remarks = member.find('remarks')
        print(f"\n{'='*80}")
        print(f"Type: {name}")
        print(f"{'='*80}")
        if summary is not None:
            text = ''.join(summary.itertext()).strip()
            print(f"Summary: {text}")
        if remarks is not None:
            text = ''.join(remarks.itertext()).strip()
            print(f"Remarks: {text[:500]}")

EOF

        <member name="M:System.IdHelpers.CreateMonotonicId(System.DateTimeOffset)">
            <summary>
            Creates a strictly monotonically increasing identifier string using 64-bit timestamp ticks
            and a 64-bit counter, formatted as a 32-character hexadecimal string (GUID-like).
            </summary>
            <param name="timestamp">The timestamp to embed in the identifier.</param>
            <returns>A new strictly monotonically increasing identifier string.</returns>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This method creates a 128-bit identifier composed of two 64-bit values:
            - High 64 bits: <see cref="P:System.DateTimeOffset.Ticks"/> from the timestamp
            - Low 64 bits: A globally monotonically increasing counter
            </para>
            <para>
            The resulting string is strictly monotonically increasing when compared lexicographically,
            which is required for keyset pagination to work correctly. Unlike <c>Guid.CreateVersion7</c>,
            which uses random bits for intra-millisecond uniqueness, this implementation guarantees
            strict ordering for all identifiers regardless of when they were created.
            </para>

<member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.AddAuthorizationFilters(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder)">
            <summary>
            Adds authorization filters to support <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.AuthorizeAttribute"/>
            on MCP server tools, prompts, and resources. This method should always be called when using
            ASP.NET Core integration to ensure proper authorization support.
            </summary>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            This method automatically configures authorization filters for all MCP server handlers. These filters respect
            authorization attributes such as <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.AuthorizeAttribute"/>
            and <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.AllowAnonymousAttribute"/>.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithDistributedCacheEventStreamStore(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions})">
            <summary>
            Registers a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore"/> as the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.ISseEventStreamStore"/> for SSE resumability.
            </summary>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <param name="configureOptions">An optional action to configure <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions"/>.</param>
            <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Distributed.IDistributedCache"/> implementation must be registered in the service collection before calling this method.
            The registered cache is automatically assigned to <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.Cache"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            To use a specific <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Distributed.IDistributedCache"/> instance instead of the one registered in DI,
            set the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.Cache"/> property in the <paramref name="configureOptions"/> callback.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions">
            <summary>
            Provides extension methods for <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder"/> to add MCP endpoints.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String)">
            <summary>
            Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
            </summary>
            <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
            <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
            <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The required MCP services have not been registered. Ensure <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})"/> has been called during application startup.</exception>

python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import re

# Parse the XML
core_xml = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml'
tree = ET.parse(core_xml)
root = tree.getroot()

# Extract all member names
members = root.findall("./members/member")
all_names = [m.get('name') for m in members if m.get('name')]

# Filter for types we're interested in
attributes_and_types = [
    name for name in all_names 
    if 'McpServer' in name and ('Attribute' in name or 'Tool' in name or 'Resource' in name)
]

print("Found McpServer-related types and attributes:")
for name in sorted(set(attributes_and_types)):
    print(f"  {name}")

EOF

================================================================================
T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute
================================================================================

Summary: Indicates that a method should be considered an .

Remarks: This attribute is applied to methods that should be exposed as tools in the Model Context Protocol. When a class
            containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
            these methods become available as tools that can be called by MCP clients.
            
            
            When methods are provided directly to , the attribute is not required.
            
            
            By default, parameters are sourced from the  dictionary, which is a collection
            of key/value pairs, and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned 's
            's . Those parameters are deserialized from the
             values in that collection. There are a few exceptions to this:
            
 

================================================================================
T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceAttribute
================================================================================

Summary: Indicates that a method or property should be considered an .

Remarks: This attribute is applied to methods or properties that should be exposed as resources in the Model Context Protocol. When a class
            containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
            these methods or properties become available as resources that can be called by MCP clients.
            
            
            When methods are provided directly to , the attribute is not required.
            
            
            Read resource requests do not contain separate arguments, only a URI. However, for templated resources, portions of that URI may be considered
            as arguments and may be bound to parameters. Further, resource methods may accept parameters that will be bound to arguments based on their type.
           

================================================================================
T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolTypeAttribute
================================================================================

Summary: Indicates that a type contains methods that should be exposed as s.

Remarks: This attribute is used to mark a class containing methods that should be automatically
            discovered and registered as s. When combined with discovery methods like
            WithToolsFromAssembly, it enables automatic registration of tools without explicitly listing each tool
            class. The attribute is not necessary when a reference to the type is provided directly to a method like WithTools.
            
            
            Within a class marked with this attribute, individual methods that should be exposed as
            tools must be marked with the .

================================================================================
T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceTypeAttribute
================================================================================

Summary: Indicates that a type contains members that should be exposed as s.

Remarks: This attribute is used to mark a class containing members that should be automatically
            discovered and registered as s. When combined with discovery methods like
            WithResourcesFromAssembly, it enables automatic registration of resources without explicitly listing each
            resource class. The attribute is not necessary when a reference to the type is provided directly to a method
            like McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithResources.
            
            
            Within a class marked with this attribute, individual members that should be exposed as

P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Name:
  Summary: Gets the name of the tool.
  Remarks: If , the method name is used.

P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Title:
  Summary: Gets or sets a human-readable title for the tool that can be displayed to users.
  Remarks: The title provides a more descriptive, user-friendly name for the tool than the tool's
            programmatic name. It is intended for display purposes and to help users understand
            the tool's purpose at a glance.
            
            
            Unlike the tool name (which follows

P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Destructive:
  Summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the tool might perform destructive updates to its environment.
  Remarks: This property is most relevant when the tool modifies its environment (ReadOnly = false).

P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Idempotent:
  Summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments
            has no additional effect on its environment.
  Remarks: This property is most relevant when the tool modifies its environment (ReadOnly = false).

P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.TaskSupport:
  Summary: Gets or sets the task support configuration for the tool.
  Remarks: When set to , clients must not attempt to invoke the tool as a task.
            When set to , clients may invoke the tool as a task or as a normal request.
            When set to , clients must invoke the tool as a task.
            
            
            If this property is not explicitly set 

P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.IconSource:
  Summary: Gets or sets the source URI for the tool's icon.
  Remarks: This value can be an HTTP/HTTPS URL pointing to an image file or a data URI with base64-encoded image data.
            When specified, a single icon will be added to the tool.
            
            
            For more advanced icon configuration (multiple icons, MIME type specification, size c

P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.OpenWorld:
  Summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this tool can interact with an "open world" of external entities.

P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.ReadOnly:
  Summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this tool does not modify its environment.
  Remarks: Read-only tools do not have side effects beyond computational resource usage.
            They don't create, update, or delete data in any system.

P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.UseStructuredContent:

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document with all this information:

Based on the XML documentation files for the MCP C# library version 1.2.0, here's a comprehensive guide to the API:

## **MCP C# Library API Summary**

### **1. MapMcp Extension Method** (ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore)

**Signature:**
```csharp
M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp(
    Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder, 
    System.String)
```

**Documentation:**
- **Purpose:** Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport
- **Parameters:**
  - `endpoints`: The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints
  - `pattern`: The route pattern prefix to map to
- **Returns:** Builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies
- **Exception:** `System.InvalidOperationException` if required MCP services haven't been registered (ensure `WithHttpTransport()` was called)
- **Protocols Supported:**
  - Streamable HTTP transport (2025-11-25 specification)
  - Legacy SSE endpoints at "/sse" and "/message" (when enabled via `HttpServerTransportOptions.EnableLegacySse`)

---

### **2. WithHttpTransport Extension Method**

**Signature:**
```csharp
M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(
    Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,
    System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})
```

**Documentation:**
- **Purpose:** Adds services necessary for `MapMcp()` to handle MCP requests and sessions using the MCP Streamable HTTP transport
- **Parameters:**
  - `builder`: The builder instance
  - `configureOptions`: Configures options for the Streamable HTTP transport (allows per-session `McpServerOptions` and running logic before/after sessions)
- **Returns:** The builder instance for chaining
- **Notes:** For HTTP server configuration (port binding, custom TLS certificates), see ASP.NET Core Minimal APIs documentation

---

### **3. AddMcpServer Extension Method**

**Signature:**
```csharp
M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions.AddMcpServer(
    Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,
    System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions})
```

**Documentation:**
- **Purpose:** Adds the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to the service collection with default options
- **Parameters:**
  - `services`: The service collection to add the server to
  - `configureOptions`: Optional callback to configure `McpServerOptions`
- **Returns:** `IMcpServerBuilder` for further configuration

---

### **4. AddAuthorizationFilters Extension Method**

**Signature:**
```csharp
M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.AddAuthorizationFilters(
    Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder)
```

**Documentation:**
- **Purpose:** Adds authorization filters to support `[Authorize]` attributes on MCP server tools, prompts, and resources
- **Important:** Should always be called when using ASP.NET Core integration
- **Returns:** The builder instance for chaining
- **Supported Attributes:** Respects `[Authorize]` and `[AllowAnonymous]` attributes

---

### **5. McpServerToolAttribute** 

**Type:** `T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute`

**Purpose:** Marks a method as an MCP tool to be exposed to clients

**Supported Properties:**

| Property | Type | Default | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|---------|
| `Name` | string | Method name | Gets the name of the tool |
| `Title` | string | null | Human-readable title for display to users |
| `Destructive` | bool | false | Indicates if tool performs destructive updates |
| `Idempotent` | bool | false | Indicates if repeated calls with same args have no additional effect |
| `ReadOnly` | bool | false | Tool doesn't modify environment (no side effects beyond computation) |
| `TaskSupport` | enum | (default) | Configuration for task support: Disabled, Optional, Required |
| `IconSource` | string | null | HTTP/HTTPS URL or base64 data URI for tool icon |
| `OpenWorld` | bool | false | Tool can interact with open world of external entities |
| `UseStructuredContent` | bool | false | Report output schema for structured content |
| `OutputSchemaType` | Type | null | Type to generate tool's output schema from |

**Usage Example Pattern:**
```csharp
[McpServerTool(Name = "myTool", Title = "My Tool", Description = "...")]
public string MyMethod(string param1) { ... }
```

---

### **6. McpServerResourceAttribute**

**Type:** `T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceAttribute`

**Purpose:** Marks a method or property as an MCP resource to be exposed to clients

**Supported Properties:**

| Property | Type | Default | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|---------|
| `Name` | string | Method/property name | Gets/sets the name of the resource |
| `Title` | string | null | Gets/sets the title of the resource |
| `MimeType` | string | null | Gets/sets the MIME (media) type |
| `UriTemplate` | string | null | Resource URI template; if null, derived from name and parameter names |
| `IconSource` | string | null | HTTP/HTTPS URL or base64 data URI for resource icon |

**URI Template Notes:**
- If template includes parameters → "resource template" (listed in `list_resourceTemplates`)
- If template doesn't include parameters → "direct resource"

**Usage Example Pattern:**
```csharp
[McpServerResource(Name = "myResource", UriTemplate = "files/{id}")]
public string ReadResource(string id) { ... }
```

---

### **7. McpServerToolTypeAttribute**

**Type:** `T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolTypeAttribute`

**Purpose:** Marks a class as containing multiple tool methods for automatic discovery

**Use Case:** Used with `WithToolsFromAssembly()` to auto-discover and register all `[McpServerTool]` methods in marked classes

---

### **8. McpServerResourceTypeAttribute**

**Type:** `T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceTypeAttribute`

**Purpose:** Marks a class as containing multiple resource members for automatic discovery

**Use Case:** Used with `WithResourcesFromAssembly()` to auto-discover and register all `[McpServerResource]` members in marked classes

---

### **9. WithTools Extension Methods (Registration)**

**Signatures:**
```csharp
// From type with reflection
WithTools<TToolType>(IMcpServerBuilder, JsonSerializerOptions)

// From instance
WithTools<TToolType>(IMcpServerBuilder, TToolType instance, JsonSerializerOptions)

// From enumerable
WithTools(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<McpServerTool>)

// From type collection
WithTools(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<Type>, JsonSerializerOptions)

// From assembly
WithToolsFromAssembly(IMcpServerBuilder, Assembly, JsonSerializerOptions)
```

**Features:**
- Discovers all public and non-public methods with `[McpServerTool]` attribute
- For instance methods: creates new instance per invocation
- Automatically handles method parameters marshalling from JSON parameters

---

### **10. WithResources Extension Methods (Registration)**

**Signatures:**
```csharp
// From type with reflection
WithResources<TResourceType>(IMcpServerBuilder)

// From instance
WithResources<TResourceType>(IMcpServerBuilder, TResourceType instance)

// From enumerable
WithResources(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<McpServerResource>)

// From type collection
WithResources(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<Type>)

// From assembly
WithResourcesFromAssembly(IMcpServerBuilder, Assembly)
```

**Features:**
- Discovers all public and non-public methods/properties with `[McpServerResource]` attribute
- For instance members: creates new instance per invocation
- URI parameters are automatically bound to method parameters

---

### **11. McpServerTool Type**

**Type:** `T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool`

**Purpose:** Abstract base class representing an invocable MCP tool for servers

**Key Static Create Methods:**
```csharp
McpServerTool.Create(MethodInfo, Object, McpServerToolCreateOptions)
McpServerTool.Create(System.Delegate, McpServerToolCreateOptions)
McpServerTool.Create(AIFunction, McpServerToolCreateOptions)
```

**Can be added to:**
- `McpServerOptions.ToolCollection` directly
- Discovered via `[McpServerTool]` attributes on methods

---

### **12. McpServerResource Type**

**Type:** `T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource`

**Purpose:** Abstract base class representing an invocable MCP resource for servers

**Key Static Create Methods:**
```csharp
McpServerResource.Create(MethodInfo, Func<RequestContext<ReadResourceRequestParams>, Object>, McpServerResourceCreateOptions)
McpServerResource.Create(System.Delegate, McpServerResourceCreateOptions)
McpServerResource.Create(AIFunction, McpServerResourceCreateOptions)
```

**Properties:**
- `IsTemplated`: Whether resource URI contains template parameters
- `Metadata`: Protocol resource template/resource metadata
- `ProtocolResourceTemplate`: ResourceTemplate for list_resourceTemplates responses
- `ProtocolResource`: Resource for read_resource responses

---

### **13. Common Extension Methods for Handlers**

```csharp
WithCallToolHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<CallToolRequestParams, CallToolResult>)
WithListToolsHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<ListToolsRequestParams, ListToolsResult>)
WithListResourceTemplatesHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<ListResourceTemplatesRequestParams, ListResourceTemplatesResult>)
WithReadResourceHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<ReadResourceRequestParams, ReadResourceResult>)
WithListResourcesHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<ListResourcesRequestParams, ListResourcesResult>)
WithSubscribeToResourcesHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<SubscribeRequestParams, EmptyResult>)
WithUnsubscribeFromResourcesHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<UnsubscribeRequestParams, EmptyResult>)
```

---

### **14. Typical Configuration Flow**

```csharp
// 1. Add MCP Server
services.AddMcpServer(options => { /* configure */ })

// 2. Configure HTTP Transport
    .WithHttpTransport(options => { /* configure */ })

// 3. Add Authorization (if using ASP.NET Core)
    .AddAuthorizationFilters()

// 4. Register Tools
    .WithTools<MyToolsClass>(serializerOptions)

// 5. Register Resources  
    .WithResources<MyResourcesClass>()

// In Minimal API endpoints:
app.MapMcp("/mcp")  // Maps to /mcp path
```

---

### **File Locations:**
- **AspNetCore Extensions:** `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.xml`
- **Core Attributes & Types:** `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml`
- **Main Extensions:** `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml`

```csharp
[McpServerResource(Name = "myResource", UriTemplate = "files/{id}")]
public string ReadResource(string id) { ... }
```

---

### **7. McpServerToolTypeAttribute**

**Type:** `T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolTypeAttribute`

**Purpose:** Marks a class as containing multiple tool methods for automatic discovery

**Use Case:** Used with `WithToolsFromAssembly()` to auto-discover and register all `[McpServerTool]` methods in marked classes

---

### **8. McpServerResourceTypeAttribute**

**Type:** `T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceTypeAttribute`

**Purpose:** Marks a class as containing multiple resource members for automatic discovery

**Use Case:** Used with `WithResourcesFromAssembly()` to auto-discover and register all `[McpServerResource]` members in marked classes

---

### **9. WithTools Extension Methods (Registration)**

**Signatures:**
```csharp
// From type with reflection
WithTools<TToolType>(IMcpServerBuilder, JsonSerializerOptions)

// From instance
WithTools<TToolType>(IMcpServerBuilder, TToolType instance, JsonSerializerOptions)

// From enumerable
WithTools(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<McpServerTool>)

// From type collection
WithTools(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<Type>, JsonSerializerOptions)

// From assembly
WithToolsFromAssembly(IMcpServerBuilder, Assembly, JsonSerializerOptions)
```

**Features:**
- Discovers all public and non-public methods with `[McpServerTool]` attribute
- For instance methods: creates new instance per invocation
- Automatically handles method parameters marshalling from JSON parameters

---

### **10. WithResources Extension Methods (Registration)**

**Signatures:**
```csharp
// From type with reflection
WithResources<TResourceType>(IMcpServerBuilder)

// From instance
WithResources<TResourceType>(IMcpServerBuilder, TResourceType instance)

// From enumerable
WithResources(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<McpServerResource>)

// From type collection
WithResources(IMcpServerBuilder, IEnumerable<Type>)

// From assembly
WithResourcesFromAssembly(IMcpServerBuilder, Assembly)
```

**Features:**
- Discovers all public and non-public methods/properties with `[McpServerResource]` attribute
- For instance members: creates new instance per invocation
- URI parameters are automatically bound to method parameters

---

### **11. McpServerTool Type**

**Type:** `T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool`

**Purpose:** Abstract base class representing an invocable MCP tool for servers

**Key Static Create Methods:**
```csharp
McpServerTool.Create(MethodInfo, Object, McpServerToolCreateOptions)
McpServerTool.Create(System.Delegate, McpServerToolCreateOptions)
McpServerTool.Create(AIFunction, McpServerToolCreateOptions)
```

**Can be added to:**
- `McpServerOptions.ToolCollection` directly
- Discovered via `[McpServerTool]` attributes on methods

---

### **12. McpServerResource Type**

**Type:** `T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource`

**Purpose:** Abstract base class representing an invocable MCP resource for servers

**Key Static Create Methods:**
```csharp
McpServerResource.Create(MethodInfo, Func<RequestContext<ReadResourceRequestParams>, Object>, McpServerResourceCreateOptions)
McpServerResource.Create(System.Delegate, McpServerResourceCreateOptions)
McpServerResource.Create(AIFunction, McpServerResourceCreateOptions)
```

**Properties:**
- `IsTemplated`: Whether resource URI contains template parameters
- `Metadata`: Protocol resource template/resource metadata
- `ProtocolResourceTemplate`: ResourceTemplate for list_resourceTemplates responses
- `ProtocolResource`: Resource for read_resource responses

---

### **13. Common Extension Methods for Handlers**

```csharp
WithCallToolHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<CallToolRequestParams, CallToolResult>)
WithListToolsHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<ListToolsRequestParams, ListToolsResult>)
WithListResourceTemplatesHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<ListResourceTemplatesRequestParams, ListResourceTemplatesResult>)
WithReadResourceHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<ReadResourceRequestParams, ReadResourceResult>)
WithListResourcesHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<ListResourcesRequestParams, ListResourcesResult>)
WithSubscribeToResourcesHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<SubscribeRequestParams, EmptyResult>)
WithUnsubscribeFromResourcesHandler(IMcpServerBuilder, McpRequestHandler<UnsubscribeRequestParams, EmptyResult>)
```

---

### **14. Typical Configuration Flow**

```csharp
// 1. Add MCP Server
services.AddMcpServer(options => { /* configure */ })

// 2. Configure HTTP Transport
    .WithHttpTransport(options => { /* configure */ })

// 3. Add Authorization (if using ASP.NET Core)
    .AddAuthorizationFilters()

// 4. Register Tools
    .WithTools<MyToolsClass>(serializerOptions)

// 5. Register Resources  
    .WithResources<MyResourcesClass>()

// In Minimal API endpoints:
app.MapMcp("/mcp")  // Maps to /mcp path
```

---

### **File Locations:**
- **AspNetCore Extensions:** `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.xml`
- **Core Attributes & Types:** `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml`
- **Main Extensions:** `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml`

index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-f0fd1bb1f0ed4ee69837e3cbf06fa355/McpHttpServer/Resources/ConfigResources.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,60 @@
+using System.ComponentModel;
+using System.Text.Json;
+using ModelContextProtocol.Server;
+
+namespace McpHttpServer.Resources;
+
+[McpServerResourceType]
+public sealed class ConfigResources
+{
+    private static readonly JsonSerializerOptions JsonOptions = new() { WriteIndented = true };
+
+    /// <summary>
+    /// A direct resource (no URI parameters) that returns application configuration.
+    /// Clients discover it via resources/list and read it via resources/read.
+    /// </summary>
+    [McpServerResource(Name = "app-config", UriTemplate = "config://app",
+        Title = "Application Configuration", MimeType = "application/json"),
+     Description("Returns the current application configuration as JSON.")]
+    public static string GetAppConfig()
+    {
+        var config = new
+        {
+            Version = "1.0.0",
+            Environment = "Production",
+            Features = new { DarkMode = true, BetaAccess = false },
+            MaxRetries = 3,
+            TimeoutSeconds = 30
+        };
+
+        return JsonSerializer.Serialize(config, JsonOptions);
+    }
+
+    /// <summary>
+    /// A resource template with a URI parameter. The {name} placeholder makes this
+    /// a template that clients discover via resources/list_resourceTemplates.
+    /// </summary>
+    [McpServerResource(Name = "feature-flag", UriTemplate = "config://features/{name}",
+        Title = "Feature Flag Lookup", MimeType = "application/json"),
+     Description("Returns the status of a specific feature flag by name.")]
+    public static string GetFeatureFlag(string name)
+    {
+        var flags = new Dictionary<string, bool>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
+        {
+            ["DarkMode"] = true,
+            ["BetaAccess"] = false,
+            ["NewDashboard"] = true,
+            ["ExperimentalApi"] = false
+        };
+
+        var result = new
+        {
+            Name = name,
+            Enabled = flags.TryGetValue(name, out var enabled) && enabled,
+            Found = flags.ContainsKey(name)
+        };
+
+        return JsonSerializer.Serialize(result, JsonOptions);
+    }
+}
+

data.content: Server is running. Now let me send MCP protocol requests to test all capabilities.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Initialize and list tools
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}}}' | head -c 2000
echo "---"
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $(curl -s -D- -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -i mcp-session-id | cut -d' ' -f2 | tr -d '\r')" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/list","params":{}}' | head -c 2000

data.content: Tools work. Let me test resources and a tool call.

arguments.command:
# Get a session ID first
SESSION=$(curl -s -D- -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream' \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -i mcp-session-id | cut -d' ' -f2 | tr -d '\r')
echo "Session: $SESSION"

# List resources
echo "=== resources/list ==="
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $SESSION" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"resources/list","params":{}}' 2>/dev/null

echo -e "\n=== resources/read (app-config) ==="
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $SESSION" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"resources/read","params":{"uri":"config://app"}}' 2>/dev/null

echo -e "\n=== resources/read (feature-flag DarkMode) ==="
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $SESSION" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":4,"method":"resources/read","params":{"uri":"config://features/DarkMode"}}' 2>/dev/null

echo -e "\n=== tools/call (echo) ==="
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $SESSION" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":5,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"Hello MCP!"}}}' 2>/dev/null

# Get a session ID first
SESSION=$(curl -s -D- -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream' \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -i mcp-session-id | cut -d' ' -f2 | tr -d '\r')
echo "Session: $SESSION"

# List resources
echo "=== resources/list ==="
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $SESSION" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"resources/list","params":{}}' 2>/dev/null

echo -e "\n=== resources/read (app-config) ==="
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $SESSION" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"resources/read","params":{"uri":"config://app"}}' 2>/dev/null

echo -e "\n=== resources/read (feature-flag DarkMode) ==="
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5921/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \

data.content:
All endpoints verified. Here's a summary of the project structure:

```
McpHttpServer/
├── Program.cs                     ← Wires up DI, HTTP transport, MapMcp("/mcp")
├── Tools/DevTools.cs              ← [McpServerTool] methods (echo, add, get_timestamp)
└── Resources/ConfigResources.cs   ← [McpServerResource] methods (app-config, feature-flag)
```

**Key patterns demonstrated:**

| Concept | How |
|---|---|
| **HTTP transport** | `.WithHttpTransport()` + `app.MapMcp("/mcp")` |
| **Direct resource** | `UriTemplate = "config://app"` — no parameters, appears in `resources/list` |
| **Resource template** | `UriTemplate = "config://features/{name}"` — parameterized, appears in `resources/list_resourceTemplates` |
| **Tools** | `[McpServerTool]` on static methods, auto-discovered via `WithTools<DevTools>()` |

Clients connect by POSTing JSON-RPC to `http://<host>/mcp` using the MCP Streamable HTTP transport. The server returns the `Mcp-Session-Id` header on `initialize`, which clients include in subsequent requests.
